 When installing IBM WebSphere Transformation Extender (WTX) Design Studio 8.4.1.1 on the Windows platform over the top of an IBM WTX 8.4.1.0 installation, the COBOL Copybook importer may be missing after the upgrade. 

SYMPTOM
The COBOL Copybook importer option no longer appears in the list of available importers.


CAUSE
Some files are not correctly overwritten / modified during the upgrade.

   problem will occur even if you have specified

   KIXREVSCB

per_question.reference: Yes, there is a specific format for COBOL copybooks to be used in IBM WebSphere Transformation Extender (WTX). In the context provided, it is mentioned that when installing IBM WTX Design Studio 8.4.1.1 over an existing installation, the COBOL Copybook importer may be missing after the upgrade. This issue occurs because some files are not correctly overwritten or modified during the upgrade process. per_question.user_input: Error while installing SpSS modeler trial version I have downloaded the SPSS modeller trial version (18.1) (64 bit) and trying to install the same. I get the following error 1152: Error extracting modelerClient64.msi to the temporary location
retrieved_contexts:
Other possible causes of this error:
- Available space within the temporary location noted by the installer.
- Ability to write in the temporary location noted by the installer.


ANSWER
Transfer the files again, resolved the issue. 

RELATED INFORMATION
 Need more help? Our Modeler forum is Live! [https://developer.ibm.com/answers/topics/modeler/?smartspace=predictive-analytics]

TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
 What causes an 1152 error during extraction of Modeler self extracting executable? 

CAUSE
When an IBM SPSS compressed image is launched, files compressed within it are extracted to the temporary location assigned to the signed in user i.e. /Documents and settings/... or /users.
The possibility of these files downloaded improperly exists. You can use a tool such
as MD5 to verify the file content or simply attempt downloading a second time and test.

Once that you have confirmed that Modeler Client is operational, repeat the steps to extract the .msi file for Modeler Client Fix Pack 1. Please note that it is highly recommended to delete the contents of the temporary folder as the Fix Pack and Client installations use the same file names. This is to prevent the accidental copying of the Client installation instead of the Fix Pack install.

* Run the installer executable (SPSS_Modeler_141_win64.exe) as normal. When it finishes extracting and decompressing the .msi file and the end-user is required to click 'Continue', minimise this window. 

* Switch to the %temp% folder and locate the folder with the 'ModelerClient64.msi' file. Copy ALL contents within this folder and paste it to a local folder (e.g. C:\Temp\Modeler\ClientInstall). 

* Once copied, switch back to the installation wizard and hit 'Cancel'.

C:\>SPSS_Statistics_24_win64.exe /b"C:\MSItest" 


3. Notice that running this command will launch the InstallShield Wizard. Let this run until the .msi has been extracted. You will see "Extracting: IBM SPSS Statistics 24.msi" message from the installer. You do not need to proceed with the full installation. 

Using Installation Media (CD/DVD)
1. Insert the disk.
2. IF prompted to install the software, select 'Cancel'.
3. Browse to the media location. 
4. Open the 'SPSSStatistics' folder.

* Once copied, switch back to the installation wizard and hit 'Cancel'. 

Step 2: Use the command prompt to execute the 'MsiExec' command: 

* Open a command prompt window as an Administrator and run the following command: (substitute <path> with the location of your .msi file) 

MsiExec.exe /i "<path>\ModelerClient.msi" /qn /L*v logfile.txt INSTALLDIR="C:\Program Files\IBM\SPSS\Modeler\14" LICENSETYPE="Network" LSHOST="mylicserver"

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
 

Having a 64-bit install of Modeler and a 32-bit installation of Statistics is not a supported configuration by IBM SPSS. In order to use the SPSS Statistics nodes from within Modeler, you will need to install the 64-bit version of Statistics. 

This is because the 32-bit API's of SPSS Statistics cannot be loaded by the 64-bit installation of SPSS Modeler. 

 

This limitation is expected to be documented in future releases of IBM SPSS Modeler.

(C)
1) Reset the date/time on the computer.
2) Start the IBM SPSS product.

IF the error message persists, THEN go to (D)
IF the error not longer presents, then the problem has been resolved.

(D)
1) Delete (or rename) the file named 'lservrc' found in program installation directory of the IBM SPSS product. 
2) License the IBM SPSS product using the License Authorization Wizard and your product authorization code.

If you have purchased Modeler Server, please contact IBM SPSS customer services and obtain a Modeler Client code that has the server enablement option checked and apply that code to your Modeler Client installation. After doing this, the server login option should be enabled after you restart the client application.

Details of how to contact customer services can be found here:

http://www.ibm.com/software/analytics/spss/ [http://www.ibm.com/software/analytics/spss/]

- Single user/Site license Installation
This is applicable to Modeler\Clementine & SPSS Client, Modeler 14.0 Server & SPSS 18 Server and below or Modeler\Clementine & SPSS Batch, AMOS and SPSS Text Analysis for Surveys.
per_question.response: The 1152 error during the extraction of the Modeler self-extracting executable can be caused by issues related to the available space or the ability to write in the temporary location assigned to the user. To resolve the issue, it is recommended to ensure there is sufficient space and permissions in the temporary folder, and if necessary, attempt downloading the files again to verify their integrity.
per_question.reference: The error 1152 that you are experiencing while trying to install the SPSS Modeler trial version is likely due to the files being extracted improperly. The solution provided in the context is to transfer the files again to resolve the issue. Make sure that there is enough space in the temporary location noted by the installer and that you have the ability to write in that location. Verify the file content using a tool such as MD5 or try downloading the files again and testing the installation process.

per_question.user_input:
How to resolve the StaleConnectionException in WebSphere Application Server? I see this exception in WebSphere Application Server J2c and RRA traces:

WSRdbManagedC > preTestConnection Entry WSRdbManagedConnectionImpl@51a507f2 query: SELECT 1 FROM DUAL WSRdbManagedC 3 Exception java.sql.SQLException: ORA-02396: exceeded maximum idle time, please connect again DSRA0010E: SQL State = 61000, Error Code = 2,396 at oracle.jdbc.driver.T4CTTIoer.processError(T4CTTIoer.java:440) at 
...
Entry java.sql.SQLException: ORA-01012: not logged on ..... OracleDataSto < findMappingClass return Exit class com.ibm.websphere.ce.cm.StaleConnectionException
retrieved_contexts:
RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Steps to resolve the problem: 

1. Stopped WAS hosting the WorkplaceXT
2. Removed any additional jar files from the <install_path>/IBM/WebShpere/AppServer/lib/ext directory so that the directory contained only two jar files: 

- authentication-websphere.jar
- log4j_1.2.13.jar

3. Deleted WorkplaceXT folder from the WAS wstemp folder, and clear the browser cache
4. Started WebSphere Application Server

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Configure the WebSphere Application Server data source connection pool [http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/infocenter/wasinfo/v7r0/index.jsp?topic=/com.ibm.websphere.nd.multiplatform.doc/info/ae/ae/udat_conpoolset.html] so as to eliminate occurrences of StaleConnectionException :

* Use the Oracle 10g driver file.  1. Log on to the administrative console and navigate to Environment > WebSphere Variables.
       
       
    2. Select the variable ORACLE_JDBC_DRIVER_PATH.
       
       
    3. Change the variable value to the folder that contains the Oracle 10g driver (ojdbc14.jar).
       
       
    4. Save the configuration.
       
       
    5. Restart the server.

CAUSE

QUESTION
 How do I export and import private keys between the same or different Hardware Security Module (HSM) enabled IBM WebSphere DataPower SOA Appliance? 

ANSWER
HSM-enabled DataPower appliances support the export of private keys using the crypto-export command. For key export to work, various conditions must be met:

Second, the key to be exported must be exportable. The exportability of keys is immutable. It is determined at keygen time, and it is controlled by that command's exportable parameter. If a key was created outside of the appliance (not using keygen), then it is always considered exportable.

As for the certificate configuration on DataPower, the appliance will reference the actual key and certificate files stored in the cert: directory by file name. Navigation in the WebGUI to the SSL Proxy Profile -> Crypto Profile -> Crypto Key and Certificate objects will show the current files referenced by the configuration.

[/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21500046&aid=3] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21500046&aid=3]

Once all of this has been done, then private keys may move from system to system with crypto-export and crypto-import. 

Note that the non-HSM appliance can export keys immediately at keygen time, but never at a later time. 

For additional information refer to the online Info Center [http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/infocenter/wsdatap/v3r8m2/index.jsp?topic=%2Fxi50%2Fwelcome.htm] and search for "PIN entry device".
. If the goal is to move exported keys from one appliance to another one, then all of the steps in this section must be followed :-

Each HSM has a special key inside of it, the key-wrapping key, that is used to encrypt exported private keys (and to decrypt imported private keys). If the goal is to restore exported keys to the same appliance, then you don't need to worry about hsm-clone-kwk, red keys, or the hsm-domain parameter. That is because the key-wrapping key at import time will already match the key-wrapping key at export time (since the HSM device is the same)

* HSMs must be initialized and in the same key sharing domain on exporting and importing machines 
 * The private key in question must be marked exportable at keygen time (see keygen's exportable option) 
 * HSMs on exporting and importing machines must share internal key-wrapping keys (see hsm-clone-kwk command). A key-wrapping key is a key that encrypts another key.

[/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21500046&aid=3] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21500046&aid=3]


For example, your DataPower configuration may currently reference the existing files cert:///current-key.pem and cert:///current-cert.pem.

To export a private key from DataPower Gateway Appliance, you can use the `crypto-export` command. However, to successfully export a private key, the following conditions must be met:

1. Ensure that the HSMs on both the exporting and importing machines are initialized and in the same key sharing domain.
2. The private key you want to export must be marked as exportable at the time of key generation.
3. The HSMs on both machines must share internal key-wrapping keys. These keys encrypt the exported private keys and decrypt imported private keys.
4. If you are moving keys from one appliance to another, synchronize the key-wrapping keys using the `hsm-clone-kwk` command on both appliances.

Follow these steps to successfully export a private key from a DataPower Gateway Appliance using the `crypto-export` command.

per_question.user_input: When should I use conversation sharing in DataPower MQ queue managers? MQ version 7.0 introduced a new feature, conversation sharing. Are there any special values on DataPower when configuring conversation sharing?
retrieved_contexts:
The default setting of sharing conversations (SHARECNV) value is zero in DataPower mq-qm object. The same default value for MQ SVRCONN channel is 10. The sharing conversations value is 
negotiated between MQ server and DataPower and the lower value takes effect. However, in current versions of the DataPower firmware, the sharing conversations setting of 1 is treated as 0 when negotiating with MQ server.

For DataPower firmware releases 7.0.0.x, 7.1.0.x, 7.2.0.x and earlier, one should follow case #1 and use a negotiated sharing conversations value of 0. If you configure these firmware versions with case #2 or case #3, DataPower device may reload or hang due to connection instability and generate many MQ errors with Reason Code - 2009 and 2059.

Set a value of 1 on the Sharing Conversations attribute and select "on" in Share Single Conversation attribute of the mq-qm object in DataPower as shown in the following picture and a value of 1 on IBM WebSphere MQ SVRCONN setting.

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
IBM WebSphere MQ server version 7.0 and later releases provide sharing conversations (SHARECNV) attribute on SVRCONN channel that specifies the maximum number of conversations that can share each TCP/IP channel instance. This feature can be configured in DataPower as it uses client connection with queue manager SVRCONN channel.

There are three use cases to configure Sharing Conversations in DataPower mq-qm object: 

Case #1 : The negotiated sharing conversations value is 0 - The channel runs in a mode similar to WebSphere MQ V6 and does not uses features such as

· Administrator stop-quiesce
· Heartbeating 
· Read ahead 
· Client asynchronous consume

. The use of fixed buffer pool for small and large messages can deplete MQ server's allocated buffer and contribute to unexpected termination of MQ SVRCONN channel instance and may terminate the queue manager. In such a situation, using a negotiated sharing conversations value of 1 as in case #2 will benefit DataPower mq-qm object as it will use IBM WebSphere MQ Version 7 Read Ahead feature and the new buffer management feature for MQGET API call

Since DataPower uses a connection pool in processing MQ messages, there are no additional benefits of using sharing conversations with the mq-qm object. However, in situations when mixed message sizes are used by the same mq-qm object, using case #2 will benefit mq-qm object as it will use new buffer management feature of IBM WebSphere MQ Version 7 when making MQGET API call.

· Administrator stop-quiesce
· Heartbeating 
· Read ahead 
· Client asynchronous consume

Set a value of 0 or 1 on the Sharing Conversations attribute of the mq-qm object in DataPower to disable sharing conversations regardless of the IBM WebSphere MQ SVRCONN setting.

Case #2: The negotiated sharing conversations value is 1 - The channel supports IBM WebSphere MQ V7 and later release features as outlined in case #1, but each TCP/IP channel instance has a single conversation.

Case #3: The negotiated sharing conversations value is 2 or more - The channel supports IBM WebSphere MQ 7 and later release features and each TCP/IP channel instance supports 2 or more conversations.

Set a value of 2 or more on the Sharing Conversations attribute of the mq-qm object in DataPower and on the MQ SVRCONN channel.

Cases #2 and #3 can be used in firmware releases 7.5.0.x, 7.5.1.x and later. We suggest to use a value of 10 or less in Sharing Conversations attribute of the mq-qm object and verify the configuration empirically to make sure that IBM WebSphere MQ server can sustain load from DataPower with the negotiated Sharing Conversations of 10.
per_question.response: You should use conversation sharing in DataPower MQ queue managers when configuring firmware releases 7.5.0.x, 7.5.1.x, and later, particularly with a negotiated sharing conversations value of 10 or less. For earlier firmware releases (7.0.0.x, 7.1.0.x, 7.2.0.x), it is recommended to use a negotiated sharing conversations value of 0 to avoid connection instability and potential errors.
per_question.reference:
Conversation sharing in DataPower MQ queue managers should be used when negotiating the sharing conversations value. It is beneficial to configure the Sharing Conversations attribute with a specific value based on the negotiated value with the MQ server. 

Three different use cases for configuring sharing conversations in DataPower MQ queue managers are outlined:
1. When the negotiated sharing conversations value is 0, it is recommended to set a value of 0 or 1 on the Sharing Conversations attribute in DataPower to disable sharing conversations.
2. If the negotiated sharing conversations value is 1, it is advised to set a value of 1 on the Sharing Conversations attribute and select "on" in Share Single Conversation attribute in DataPower. 
3. When the negotiated sharing conversations value is 2 or more, it is recommended to set a value of 2 or more on the Sharing Conversations attribute.

It is important to configure the Sharing Conversations attribute appropriately to ensure optimal performance and avoid potential errors or instability in the MQ environment.

 When attempting to license an SPSS product, I am getting the following error message.

Authorization in progress ... (xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx)

License error: miscellaneous key-related error. Error sub-code: 88 

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
In general when accessing SPSS Modeler or SPSS Statistics software via a remote desktop or terminal services session a network license is required. In some configurations where the Windows host limits the number of active sessions to 1 a local license may still operate, but we cannot assist with attempts to achieve such a configuration.

Windows operating systems
(1) Select Start.
(2) Select 'Programs' (or 'All Programs') menu entry.
(3) Select the program folder corresponding to the product (example: 'IBM SPSS Statistics')
(4) Launch the 'License Authorization' program corresponding to the product version (example: 'IBM SPSS Statistics 24 License Authorization Wizard')
(5) Select the 'Next' button.
(6) In the lower left section of this window will appear 'Lock Code for this machine is'. Note the indicated lock code.
. If the problem persists, contact your next level of support.

Please see the documentation for your version of SPSS Modeler or SPSS Statistics for details on how to setup and use a network/concurrent license. When installing a fix pack for IBM Integration Bus (IIB) in a multi-instance environment, it is important to consider the following:

1. **Installation and Verification Tasks**: 
   - Ensure that the fix pack is compatible with your current version of IIB.
   - Follow the installation instructions provided by IBM for applying the fix pack to a multi-instance environment.
   - Verify that the installation is successful on all instances of IIB in your environment.

2. **Rollback Procedure**: 
   - Before applying the fix pack, make sure to have a backup of your current configuration and data.
   - Understand the rollback procedure in case the fix pack causes any issues.
   - Test the rollback procedure in a non-production environment to ensure it works as expected.

3. **Multi-Instance Configuration Tasks**:
   - Ensure that all instances of IIB are stopped before applying the fix pack.
   - Apply the fix pack to each instance of IIB individually to avoid any issues related to multi-instance configuration.
   - After applying the fix pack to all instances, start them one by one to ensure proper functioning.

4. **Testing After Installation**:
   - Once the fix pack is applied to all instances, perform thorough testing of your applications to ensure that they are functioning correctly.
   - Test the communication and interaction between different instances of IIB to ensure that the multi-instance setup is still working as expected.

5. **Verify Post-Installation Configurations**:
   - Double-check any custom configurations or settings that were applied to the multi-instance setup to ensure they are still in place after the fix pack installation.
   - Verify that any integration points or dependencies with other systems are not affected by the fix pack installation.

By considering these aspects, you can ensure a smooth installation process of the fix pack for IBM Integration Bus in a multi-instance environment and minimize any potential issues that may arise.

per_question.reference:
To address the issue of the Event Dashboard breaking when a filter is created with the browser language set to Japanese in WebGUI 8.1 Fix Pack 11, you can follow the steps outlined in the APAR STATUS section of the provided context.

The APAR for this issue is JR46703, which affects BPM ADVANCED on version 8.0.1.0. The problem description states that when users with Non-English locales (e.g. ja_JP) for their browsers access Process Portal, the Dashboards do not display the Pie-Chart and Task lists.

The local fix suggested is to temporarily set the browser language preference to English, access the dashboard, and then change it back to the real locale, such as Japanese. This workaround should help resolve the issue until a permanent fix is implemented.

The problem was addressed by adding a configurable flag that resolves the user's browser locale to a Portal-supported locale and properly sets the language as their User Preference. If the user's browser locale is not a Portal-supported locale, then the user's locale will default to English.

To resolve this issue permanently, you can download the fix package from FixCentral. The fix information including install instructions will be available in the readme.txt file provided with the fix package.

per_question.reference: The file db2jcc.jar is missing after an upgrade to BPM V8.5.7 CF2016.12 because IBM BPM does not transform complex IBM BPM types (such as TWObjects) to standard JDBC types that the database can process. Therefore, the SQL Connector code can only handle simple types. This limitation was not documented previously, but it will be addressed in an upcoming IBM BPM V8.5.7 cumulative fix by adding information about this limitation to the SQLParameter variable types description. In the meantime, a temporary fix is to use the Java integration feature to invoke custom Java code using JDBC java interfaces to call stored procedures.

retrieved_contexts: The output above shows that three default gateways were configured on eth0, eth1, and mgt0 which causes three "default" route to be generated (0.0.0.0/0). If the destination address of your service is 166.84.23.4, the device will randomly choose any one of the default routes as no specific route is configured. If, for example, the gateway picked is 10.238.77.1 and the gateway does not know how to route to the needed backend server ip address, this will cause unexpected connection failures

If you want your network traffic to follow a specific route, set a static route and NOT rely on the default gateway. As a general rule, the management interface should not have the default gateway set, as you generally want to restrict the connections that can be made through that interface. 

A general recommendation is to set specific static routes and only ONE default gateway that will catch all traffic not being directed by the static routes.

Based on the given context, the unexpected traffic across domains could be happening due to the configuration of cross-certification between the two domains. In the context provided, it mentions setting up cross-certification for user access and replication. 

If there is cross-certification set up between Domain A and Domain B for user access, it is possible that this cross-certification is allowing traffic to flow from Domain A to Domain B, even if the FSH_B is disabled in MPGW_B. 

To investigate further, you may need to review the cross-certification setup between the two domains and ensure that it is configured only for user access in one direction as intended. Additionally, checking the configuration of the MPGWs and the traffic routing rules within the domains may also provide insights into how the traffic is being redirected to MPGW_B despite FSH_B being disabled.

DFHAP1203I and DFHSR0601 with 0C1 in EQA10OSM after upgrading to CICS TS 5.2 Why is my CICS region failing during startup with message DFHAP1203I followed by DFHSR0601 after I upgraded to CICS Transaction Server for z/OS (CICS TS) V5.2? A system dump shows an abend 0C1 occurring in the Debug Tool for z/OS module EQA10OSM. 
I receive the following messages:
DFHAP1203I Language Environment is being initialized. 
DFHSR0601 Program interrupt occurred with system task III in control.
retrieved_contexts:
PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 You upgrade to a new release of z/OS and CICS Transaction Server fails to initialize. You see the following messages:

DFHAP1203I Language Environment is being initialized.
DFHSR0601 Program interrupt occurred with system task III in control. 

CAUSE
There is a mismatch in z/OS levels between Language Environment modules, CEECCICS and CEEPLPKA.

0C4 ABENDS0C4 BPXP018I DFHKE1800 MSGDFHSR0601 MSGDFHSR0622 5655M1500 R640 640 R400 400 R650 650 R500 500 HCI6400 HCI6500 5655-M15 5655M15 kixinfo retention32 TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 Your CICS Transaction Server for z/OS (CICS TS) region receives messages:
DFHSR0622 An attempt to overwrite the ERDSA has caused the abend which follows
DFHSR0601 Program interrupt occurred with system task TCP in control
DFHKE1800 ABNORMAL TERMINATION OF CICS IS COMPLETE

DIAGNOSING THE PROBLEM
You examine a system dump on the DFHSR0601 [http://publibfi.boulder.ibm.com/cgi-bin/bookmgr/BOOKS/dfhg4c00/1.87?ACTION=MATCHES&REQUEST=dfhsr0601&TYPE=FUZZY&SHELF=dfhasj00&DT=20070614214024&CASE=&searchTopic=TOPIC&searchText=TEXT&searchIndex=INDEX&rank=RANK&ScrollTOP=FIRSTHIT#FIRSTHIT] message and find that the running task is TCP CSTP.

From reviewing the dump, you see the running task has program checked twice. The first 0C1 is occurring in DFHPASY (base) around offset x'8E', in the middle of the copyright eyecatcher for this module.

DFHSJ0215 JVM Server OSGi framework DFHSJ0914 JVMSERVER DISABLED Language Environment Enclave LE LE/370 5655-S97 5655S97 5655S9700 R670 670 R700 700 HCI6700 5655-S97 5655S97 5655Y0400 R680 680 R800 800 HCI6800 5655-Y04 5655Y04 kixinfo TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 You install preventative maintenance to CICS Transaction Server for z/OS (CICS TS). Now when you install a JVMServer you receive DFHSJ0214 and DFHSJ0914 error messages.

Now that you know the module name and the offset, search for a known problem on the CICS Support page [http://www.ibm.com/support/entry/portal/Overview/Software/Other_Software/CICS_Transaction_Server]. Use keywords such as the following for a search argument: 'DFHAP0001 DFHAPDM 0C4'. 

RELATED INFORMATION
 Finding where a program check occurred [http://www.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/SSGMCP_4.1.0/com.ibm.cics.ts.doc/dfhs1/topics/dfhs1l8.html]

CECI ABEND0C4 ABEND 0C4 ABENDS0C4 ABEND S0C4 DFHSR0001 MSGDFHSR0001 EXECKEY 5655M1500 R640 640 R400 400 R650 650 R500 500 HCI6400 HCI6500 5655-M15 5655M15 kixinfo retention32 TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 You receive the following message in your CICS Transaction Server region: 

DFHSR0001 An abend (code 0C4/AKEA) has occurred at offset X'B0E' in program DFHECID. 

CAUSE
The startup GRPLIST contains a duplicate definition for program DFHECID with specification EXECKEY(USER).

SYMPTOM
DFHTD0101I [http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/infocenter/cicsts/v3r2/index.jsp?topic=/com.ibm.cics.ts.messages.doc/cics_mc/dfhtd/TD0101I.html] Transient Data initialization has ended.
. Once you have upgraded the system with this fix you should use the CLI command:

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 You receive a program check that has an offset X'FFFFFFFF' in a CICS Transaction Server for z/OS (CICS TS) V4.1 region. For example, DFHAP0001 An abend (code 0C4/AKEA) has occurred at offset X'FFFFFFFF' in module DFHAMP. The X'FFFFFFFF' indicates that CICS was unable establish the location of the program check. So, you would like to locate the PSW and registers in your CICS system dump.
per_question.response: The CICS region is failing during startup with messages DFHAP1203I and DFHSR0601 due to a mismatch in z/OS levels between Language Environment modules, specifically CEECCICS and CEEPLPKA. This mismatch can lead to program interrupts, as indicated by the DFHSR0601 message, which suggests that the Language Environment is not properly initialized for the upgraded CICS Transaction Server version.
per_question.reference: The issue is occurring due to a mismatch in z/OS levels between Language Environment modules, specifically CEECCICS and CEEPLPKA. To resolve this problem, ensure that there is only one instance of CEEPLPKA and CEECCICS in your library concatenations, and that they are at the correct z/OS level.

This can lead to repeated entries in the server log, such as the error message WTRN0047E, indicating that the session context contains a non-serializable object.
per_question.reference:
Based on the context provided, the Java.io.NotSerializableException typically occurs when an object that does not implement the `java.io.Serializable` interface is attempted to be serialized or deserialized. This exception can be encountered in scenarios involving Persistent sessions, PMI, and HTTPSession.

To resolve the `java.io.NotSerializableException`, ensure that all objects placed in the HTTPSession implement the `java.io.Serializable` interface. Additionally, you can use the `SessionInspectServlet` provided by WebSphere Application Server to determine which attribute of the session object references a non-serializable object.

If you are experiencing this exception in WAS, it is recommended to check the objects being stored in the session for serialization compatibility and use the `SessionInspectServlet` to identify the issue further and ensure proper implementation of the `java.io.Serializable` interface for all relevant objects.

per_question.user_input: Does IBM DataPower Gateway Appliance support the HTTP CONNECT method? Does IBM DataPower Gateway Appliance support the HTTP CONNECT method for SSL proxy requests?
retrieved_contexts:
ERROR DESCRIPTION
 *  Datapower service (i.e MPGW, WSP, etc) is configured to connect
   to backend server through an HTTP Proxy server (as configured
   in the Proxy Policy of the user agent) using the HTTP CONNECT
   method.
   However, if the HTTP CONNECT request to the proxy server,
   receives a 5xx response, Datapower might issue the following SSL
   error:
   20160711T163325.760Z [0x8120002f][ssl][error]
   ssl-client(PMYClientProfile): tid(11573565)[10.x.x.x]

Users configuring DataPower may need a way to implement this CONNECT method for SSL tunneling to a remote Proxy Server.




RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Non-SSL Proxy requests will work using either:

a) an XML Firewall when configuring in the HTTP Options tab, where the user can configure the Proxy Host and Port

b) a Multi-Protocol Gateway when configuring an HTTP Proxy Policy in the User Agent

PROBLEM SUMMARY
 *  Affected are all customers using the Multi-Protocol Gateway,
   Web Service Proxy or Web Application Firewall with persistent
   connection to the backend side.
   
   In case the backend server stops responding to HTTP GET
   requests while there are already some established TCP
   connections from the time the server worked OK, DataPower will
   automatically retry those GET requests after a timeout period.

b) a Multi-Protocol Gateway when configuring an HTTP Proxy Policy in the User Agent

SSL Proxy requests will work using the CONNECT method for SSL Tunneling when using a Multi-Protocol Gateway when configuring in a User Agent's HTTP Proxy Policy.

The XML Firewall service is not designed to use the CONNECT method for SSL Tunneling.

For DataPower versions 6.0.0 and later:

TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA
TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_SHA
TLS_RSA_WITH_NULL_SHA256
TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA256
TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_SHA256

For DataPower versions prior to 6.0.0:

TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA
TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_SHA
AES_SHA_US

(b). In WebSphere MQ channels select the same TLS protocol as DataPower uses from the channel CipherSpec.

When DataPower mq-qm object is using an SSL Proxy Profile:

When DataPower mq-qm object is using an SSL Proxy Profile:

For V6 or later: http://pic.dhe.ibm.com/infocenter/wsdatap/v6r0m0/topic/com.ibm.dp.xg.doc/mq_securingwithansslproxyprofileobject.html [http://pic.dhe.ibm.com/infocenter/wsdatap/v6r0m0/topic/com.ibm.dp.xg.doc/mq_securingwithansslproxyprofileobject.html]

ERROR DESCRIPTION
 *  Affected are customers sending HTTP GET requests to the
   multiprotocol gateway, web service proxy or web application
   firewall service.
   
   With the present implementation, users may end up in a state
   with indefinite retries for HTTP GET requests as long as
   DataPower has at least one live persistent connection to the
   backend server. After timeout interval for active connection it
   will terminate the connection and retry using remaining

datapower; retrieve; get; wsdl; web service proxy; web services proxy service; WSP; WS; GET method; GET; HTTP; HTTPS; front side handler; 7993; 7993; 7993; machine type 7993 TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 You are looking to retrieve a WSDL from a Web Service Proxy service with the IBM WebSphere DataPower XS40 or XI50 appliance

Forward Proxy XMLFW MPGW CONNECT TUNNEL TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 How to configure an IBM WebSphere DataPower SOA Appliance Service to proxy an SSL request to a remote proxy server using the CONNECT method for SSL tunneling? 

CAUSE
Many common forward proxy servers expect requests in different formats depending on if they are proxying non-SSL or SSL requests.

For example, a non-SSL request to be proxied may come in the form of GET hostname:port/URI.

As for the certificate configuration on DataPower, the appliance will reference the actual key and certificate files stored in the cert: directory by file name. Navigation in the WebGUI to the SSL Proxy Profile -> Crypto Profile -> Crypto Key and Certificate objects will show the current files referenced by the configuration.

[/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21500046&aid=3] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21500046&aid=3]
per_question.response: Yes, the IBM DataPower Gateway Appliance supports the HTTP CONNECT method for SSL proxy requests when using a Multi-Protocol Gateway configured in a User Agent's HTTP Proxy Policy. However, the XML Firewall service is not designed to use the CONNECT method for SSL tunneling.
per_question.reference: Yes, IBM DataPower Gateway Appliance does support the HTTP CONNECT method. Specifically, for SSL proxy requests, IBM DataPower Gateway Appliance supports the HTTP CONNECT method for SSL tunneling when using a Multi-Protocol Gateway and configuring in a User Agent's HTTP Proxy Policy.

per_question.user_input:
Datacap on Citrix  Hi All,

Can we operate Datacap thin clients on citrix?
retrieved_contexts: Some customers successfully operate Datacap thick clients in remote sites using Citrix or other remote access technology. IBM has not tested or sought certification with Citrix, and does not provide support for Citrix. If you deploy Datacap clients on Citrix and encounter issues, IBM may require you to reproduce the issues outside of Citrix as part of the investigation.

WAN LAN architecture deployment TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
 What are the best practices for deploying Datacap servers and clients on a wide area network (WAN)? 

ANSWER
Remote users that access Datacap over a WAN can use Taskmaster Web-based "thin clients", or FastDoc Capture operating in offline mode. Datacap thick clients (DotScan, DotEdit) and utilities (NENU, Fingerprint Maintenance Tool) require LAN communication speeds and low latency for responsive performance.

RELEASE NOTES

ABSTRACT
 You must modify the header information in the ICR_P.rrx file to run Parascript V8.0.1 with Datacap Taskmaster Capture V8.1.0 or Datacap V9.0.0. 

CONTENT
After you install Parascript FieldScript V8.0.1, you must edit the ICR_P.rrx file to run the product with Datacap Taskmaster Capture V8.1.0 or Datacap V9.0.0:

1. Navigate to the <Datacap_Installation>\RRS directory and open the ICR_P.rrx file with a text editor. 
 2. In the header information of the RRX file, replace instances of rrunner.rrx with rrunner. 
 3. Replace instances of recog_shared.rrx with recog_shared. 
 4. Save and close the file.





Cross reference information Segment Product Component Platform Version Edition Enterprise Content Management Datacap Taskmaster Capture

1. On client system open a Command Prompt window 
 2. Type
    cd c:\Datacap\Taskmaster 
 3. Then type
    dcskey.exe -i 
 4. If you are still unable to connect then Remove the client system from the domain 
 5. On the client delete the local folder of the domain user 
 6. Reconnect the client to the domain 
 7. Log into client with domain user 
 8. Repeat steps 1 through 4 and connect with DotEdit and DotScan

taskmaster web error messages TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
 Is there a setting that can disable or suppress web error messages in the Datacap Taskmaster Capture thin client? 

ANSWER
No, there is currently no setting that would allow web error messages to be suppressed or disabled. IBM Lab Services [http://www.ibm.com/software/ecm/services/] does have the ability to customize Datacap Taskmaster to do this, but the customization is not supported by IBM Support.
. Prerequisites for IBM® Datacap Version 9.1.3 Interim Fix 002

If you’re publishing it as an application, advise users to do a File > Exit to cleanly close the ICA session and to avoid leaving ghost sessions on the XenApp server. You may also want to check with Citrix to see if they have suggestions on handling cases where ICA sessions are terminated but the processes are still running.

Q. I read in the forum that the Admin client and the Designer client are not supported on Citrix. Is that correct? 
A. Correct, neither the Domino Administrator client nor the Domino Designer clients are supported on Citrix. 



ORIGINAL PUBLICATION DATE
 2011/7/1
per_question.reference: Based on the provided context, Datacap thin clients can be operated on Citrix. However, it is mentioned that IBM has not tested or sought certification with Citrix, and does not provide support for Citrix. If any issues are encountered while deploying Datacap clients on Citrix, IBM may require you to reproduce the issues outside of Citrix as part of the investigation.
